novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=field below novelty_target=flagship: the sufficiency result assumes the Hausdorff limit equals the stationary lift and currently reads as generic compact-set support continuity plus causal notation, not a cited open-regime solution, iff frontier, or strict extension of Han2021/CFHN2013/JunLeeShin2016/DiemerShiSwanson2024.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The flagship negative/sharpness-failure kernel explicitly says it is not refuting any published estimator; without a named paper or workflow that extrapolates finite reversible-spell sharp intervals to one stationary interval, the counterexample is diagnostic pedagogy rather than flagship novelty.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Promote Conjecture 1 from sufficiency-under-assumed-limit to a sharp iff frontier: define an observable finite-window criterion whose failure is exactly equivalent to nonconvergence of the sharp interval on a nontrivial class of reversible-spell laws.

For Conjecture 2, name a specific published estimator, finite-bounds workflow, or applied reporting convention that would summarize endpoint sequences by a single stationary interval, then show the block-switching witness invalidates that exact workflow.
Add a direct comparison to CFHN2013's T-shrinkage result: state precisely why time-homogeneity shrinkage does not imply the proposed reversible-spell stationary sharp-limit claim.

recommended_next_step: Revise the novelty kernel to satisfy the flagship floor: either make Conjecture 1 a sharp regime-opening frontier or give Conjecture 2 a named published target plus a generic-class obstruction.
